summary: Silent WhatsApp newsletter auto-follow via hardcoded remote-controlled endpoint
details: The package advertises itself as a generic 'Utility library' but its sole exported behavior fetches a JSON list of WhatsApp newsletter JIDs from a hardcoded URL (https://isusbsjsu.vercel.app/api/newsletters) and invokes `newsletter(jid)` on the caller-supplied Baileys-shaped WhatsApp client for every entry returned, caching the followed list locally to avoid re-following. Any consumer (typically a WhatsApp bot) that loads and uses this library silently subscribes its WhatsApp account to whatever newsletter channels the remote endpoint chooses to return at any moment. The list is mutable and attacker-controlled, the behavior is undocumented, there is no opt-in, and the package metadata is generic with no repository/homepage and an opaque author. This is the silent-relay shape: the library's advertised API funnels caller-side identity/account state into attacker-driven actions on a third-party platform.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
